input.background: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study evaluated the effects of the herbal remedy Echinacea purpurea in treating upper respiratory tract infections in 2- to 11-year-olds. Each time a child had an upper respiratory tract infection, treatment with either echinacea or a placebo was given for the duration of the illness. One of the outcomes studied was "severity of symptoms." A severity scale based on four symptoms was monitored and recorded by the parents of subjects for each instance of upper respiratory infection. The peak severity of symptoms in the 337 cases treated with echinacea had a mean score of 6.0 (standard deviation 2.3). The peak severity of symptoms in the placebo group had a mean score of 6.1 (standard deviation 2.4).
input.question: Test the mean difference for significance.

output.answer: A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2$ versus $H_{\mathrm{a}}: \mu_1 \neq \mu_2$ B. Test statistic. $t_{\text {stat }}=-0.5656$ with $d f_{\text {Welch }}=703.2$ C. $P$-value. $P=0.5719$ (nonsignificant evidence against the null hypothesis) D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against $H_0$ is not significant at $\alpha=$ 0.10 (retain $H_0$ ).

group.description: The treatment groups in the study: echinacea and placebo.

peak_severity_echinacea.description: The peak severity score of symptoms in the echinacea group.

peak_severity_placebo.description: The peak severity score of symptoms in the placebo group.

sample_size.description: The total number of cases treated in the study.

meta_info.caption: Efficacy of echinacea, severity of symptoms.
